This document defines how exactly a package claims Headless compatibility.

A package is considered compatible only if it: fulfills the requirements of docs/SPEC V1.md, adheres to v1 contracts from docs/V1 DECISIONS.md (for the parts it uses), h...kage should not be called "Headless-compatible".
MUST : no dependency on other components (packages/components/ ). MUST : domain/ does not import package:flutter/ . MUST : public API is minimal; no leakage of renderer implementation.
MUST : R is responsible for behavior/states/a11y, while visuals are delegated to a renderer capability (headless contracts). MUST : absence of a renderer capability produces a clear diagnostic error.

SHOULD (for preset/theme packages): there is a test verifying that subtree-override capability works: base theme provides capability X; subtree overrides X; lookup inside subtree returns the override.
MUST : controlled mode is not overwritten by internal state. MUST : an external controller is not disposed by the component; an internal one is disposed.

If the component uses an overlay/menu pattern: MUST : overlay via headless foundation, without Navigator/Route. MUST : lifecycle phase contract is followed (opening/open...itives or equivalently meeting their invariants.

This document answers the question: why Headless makes sense as a library , even though Flutter makes it easy to customize UI and write your own components. Related docu...s compatibility and the minimum checks required.

Headless is not "a set of buttons". Headless is a system of contracts and mechanisms that lets you: maintain consistent behavior and a unified design language across a t...redictable the system remains after 6-12 months.

When everyone writes their "own" widgets, inconsistencies creep in fast: different states (hover/pressed/focus/disabled) implemented differently, different sizes/touch t...consistencies "harder to introduce by accident".

"Customizing a widget" in an app often ends up as: if (brand == ...) inside the UI, duplicated components like ButtonA/ButtonB, copying files "per brand". Headless lets...iness code or creating parallel implementations.

In a UI system, the expensive part is not "drawing" but "not surprising". Headless enforces predictability: controlled/uncontrolled model (like TextField), clear default...rlays "by magic"), unified state priority rules.

Complex interactive components (Select/Menu/Combobox/Dialog) break not on the happy path but at the edges: focus trapping and focus return, keyboard navigation, closing...that behavior is uniform across all components.

In the headless approach, what matters is testing behavior , not pixels: state transitions, callback correctness, a11y semantics, keyboard scenarios, state invariants. W...me fragmented and regressions become inevitable.

A "set of custom widgets" typically evolves chaotically. Headless (see docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) relies on: capability contracts (ISP) instead of a single giant interface, r...ation policy instead of sudden breaking changes.

In the web world, headless UI often depends on hooks/render-props and runs into server/client boundaries (e.g., React Server Components). Flutter doesn't have that split...nderer contracts rather than "magical" patterns.

Does not impose a state manager (Riverpod/BLoC/MobX) - the library must be compatible with all approaches. Does not impose DI (get it/injectable/modularity dart) - DI st...n architecture for different brands and systems.

To lower the barrier to entry without sacrificing headless architecture: there is simple sugar (style: R Style) that compiles into overrides, there are theme defaults (M...is split into Users/Contributors + golden path.

Headless can be overkill if: single brand, small team, no requirements for a11y/keyboard/overlay complexity, UI changes rarely and is not a product differentiator, you don't plan to maintain a design system as a library.

Headless delivers maximum ROI when: brands (white-label) or different product lines, multiple teams building UI in parallel, many complex components (select/menu/dialog)...ve for years without "archaeology" and rewrites.

If you answer "yes" to most of these, Headless will almost certainly pay off: Our implementations of the same components regularly diverge. We've already made copies "pe...right way" is easier than "the accidental way".

Ниже — типовые ошибки в headless/UI библиотеках в других экосистемах. Мы фиксируем их как guardrails для Headless v1+.

Проблема: при композиции компонентов часто “теряются” props/семантика/инварианты, появляются трудноотлавливаемые баги. Решение для Headless: не нужен as . Структуру/визу...ерез render(request) + slots (Replace/Decorate).

Проблема: isLoading/isError/isHovered/... быстро дают \(2^n\) комбинаций, большинство из которых невалидны. Решение для Headless: для сложных интеракций используем FSM ;...вная нормализация/приоритеты (state resolution).

Проблема: platform mismatch и слабая кастомизация приводит к “мимо iOS/desktop”. Решение для Headless: core остаётся unstyled ; любые “material-like” дефолты — только в отдельном пакете default renderers.

Проблема: неправильная семантика/структура ломает screen readers и клавиатуру; “починим потом” почти всегда дороже. Решение для Headless: тесты v1 — behavior + a11y (keyboard/focus/semantics), а не golden.

Проблема: anchored overlays ломаются в скролл-контейнерах, при resize, при collision. Решение для Headless: overlay — это foundation механизм с позиционированием в стиле floating-ui (flip/shift/collision pipeline).
Почему это полезно: предсказуемость, тестируемость, невозможные состояния невозможны. Куда применяем: Dialog, Select/Combobox, menu‑подобные паттерны.
Почему это полезно: точечный override без переписывания всего renderer’а. Как у нас: render(request) + slots Replace/Decorate (см. docs/V1 DECISIONS.md).

Почему это полезно: разные платформы ожидают разное поведение/анимации. Как у нас: это ответственность default renderers/theme, core остаётся headless.
